title: hypericum repens var. majus
intro: Hypericum repens var. majus, also known as Creeping St. John's Wort, is a low-growing, spreading shrub native to Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, yellow flowers and woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, and its medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Hypericum repens var. majus include its ability to reduce anxiety and depression, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Hypericum repens var. majus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support mental health and promote overall well-being.
